rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.title: Screening by Coral GFP-like Chromoproteins (Coral Reefs 2012)
Probing Latent Subspaces in LLM for Security (2025/3)
SARSteer — Safe-Ablated Refusal Steering for Audio LLMs (2025/10)
Feature-Guided SAE Steering for Refusal-Rate Control (2025/11)
Rogue Scalpel — Activation Steering Compromises Safety (2025/9)

fail_reason: 7 prior works exceed the functional/semantic thresholds for FRECONV. Multiple existing methods (SARSteer, SafeSteer, Feature-Guided SAE Steering, Adversarial Activation Patching, Probing Latent Subspaces, Obfuscated Activations, Rogue Scalpel) already perform inference-time manipulation of harmful directions in LLM activation/eigenspace toward safe directions, which is functionally equivalent to FRECONV's harmful-eigenband-to-safe-eigenband remap. The 'partial energy conservation' framing does not constitute a meaningful operational difference from existing activation steering / SAE feature redirection. max_sem=0.82, max_func=0.84 — both well above 0.7 threshold across 7 of 8 retrieved works (only the coral biology source-domain paper falls below).
